mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and eases financial conditions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and 20-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, a Sahm-rule-like recession signal activates, causing risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.1 as of t-1mo, near historical Sahm trigger threshold
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger fires, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly signal a broad-based consumer and housing demand contraction that neither series alone captures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM decline AND HOUST MoM decline concurrently
failure_condition: Joint decline episodes produce 20-day forward returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, positive month-over-month INDPRO growth confirms continued industrial output momentum, supporting short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as it signals sustained corporate revenue strength before earnings revisions catch up.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with positive industrial momentum
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO prints are not statistically distinguishable from returns following negative prints (p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling sample, or effect reverses sign outside late-cycle regime.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation decelerates, real policy rates rise passively, increasing the probability of a near-term Fed pivot; markets price this in advance, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY inflation decelerating for 3+ consecutive months
regime_hint: disinflationary tightening plateau near terminal rate
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects continued labor market expansion and consumer income support, which sustains corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS MoM change and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 across any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in HOUST exceeding 10% signals a sharp deterioration in interest-rate-sensitive housing demand, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1354 thousand units as of t-2mo
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing stress
failure_condition: Triggered episodes show 60-day forward returns positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The joint level of FEDFUNDS relative to CPIAUCSL inflation defines the real policy rate; a high positive real rate combined with elevated FEDFUNDS signals restrictive conditions that suppress short-horizon equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days around key data releases.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with positive real rates
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant difference (p > 0.05) between high real-rate and low real-rate regimes over a 2-year rolling sample, or Rank IC < 0.03.
